title: The origin of Kaworus piano
wikitext:
[]: The origin of Kaworu's piano             Quote Post by qwdc » Sun Jun 23, 2013 5:12 am Something that caught my attention was the fact that Kaworu plays piano in 3.0 but not in any anime episode I recall. "Death" does prove that he has at least some background with musical instruments, but we never see him play the piano specifically. Kaworu does play piano the first time he meets Shinji in the manga, which makes me wonder if the piano scene of 3.0 was a nod the manga. I am a bit new to the forums, so I'm not sure if this is the right place to post this topic. This is just something I wanted to bring up because I thought it was interesting.

[]:             Quote Post by Rena » Sun Jun 23, 2013 6:05 am Kaworu only appeared for one episode in NGE, and nothing was mention of him being able to play piano in episode 24. The drafts said Kaworu could play piano, though. I thought that for Rebuild he just learned over the 14 year time skip, and playing a duet with Shinji helped them sync so they could pilot Unit 13 together.

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Sun Jun 23, 2013 9:13 am I can just imagine Kaworu, Rei Q, Fuyutsuki, and Gendo all working together to lug that Piano all the way to where it is.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Sun Jun 23, 2013 10:04 am TheFriskyIan wrote: I can just imagine Kaworu, Rei Q, Fuyutsuki, and Gendo all working together to lug that Piano all the way to where it is.

[]:             Quote Post by driftking18594 » Sun Jun 23, 2013 1:17 pm Rena wrote: I thought that for Rebuild he just learned over the 14 year time skip

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sun Jun 23, 2013 2:35 pm TheFriskyIan wrote: I can just imagine Kaworu, Rei Q, Fuyutsuki, and Gendo all working together to lug that Piano all the way to where it is.

[]:             Quote Post by tzigi » Sun Jun 23, 2013 2:36 pm TMBounty_Hunter wrote: Or Kaworu with a megaphone directing 20 Rei clones

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sun Jun 23, 2013 3:27 pm The existence of the piano, and the reconcreted floor of the cage, tells us a lot. At the very least, it tells us that Nerv HQ was being supplied and worked on before it reached its present condition. While Kaworu could have simply flown down to the surface and salvaged for a piano amidst the wreck of Tokyi-3, I tend to think Gendo or Seele got him one.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun Jun 23, 2013 4:31 pm Guys, remember that we're talking about the guy who in NGE could make two fucking Evangelions levitate with his hands in his pockets, putting that piano down the cage is like morning exercises for him!

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Sun Jun 23, 2013 4:44 pm ElMariachi wrote: Guys, remember that we're talking about the guy who in NGE could make two fucking Evangelions levitate with his hands in his pockets, putting that piano down the cage is like morning exercises for him!

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sun Jun 23, 2013 5:26 pm Kaworu wished a piano into existence when he felt he needed one - just like in the manga. There's no need for any other explanation.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Sun Jun 23, 2013 5:30 pm I hope this thread doesn't turn into all jokes.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun Jun 23, 2013 5:54 pm OK, more seriously, I recall a live interview of Okada Toshio, one of the co-founders of GAINAX and he said that Evangelion(both NGE and the Rebuild) are "a scene oriented work" instead of "scenario oriented", meaning that the "how" of a scene isn't really important compared of "what" it makes us feel. He illustrated that with the example of neo-NERV HQ and Fuyutsuki in 3.0 : Toshio Okada wrote: To me, Evangelion is a scene oriented work. It isn't a work which focuses on the scenario as its top priority. What I mean by that is that it's not a work that is meant to "show this certain story" but instead it's a work with a stockpile of "scenes to be shown" is pieced and ordered into a work. It's a dance song. You don't only have to listen to the song but see the dance moves. But the moves don't necessarily have to match with the lyrics. I believe Evangelion isn't a story where the concern went into making sure that what is shown matches the story itself, but more importantly glued well together with style and entertaining elements. In Q, Nerv, being the evil side and Wille of the justice is facing one another. But there are only two people at Nerv, Gendo and Fuyutsuki. So people wonder 'does Fuyutsuki do all the work?' But that doesn't really matter because of course it's scene oriented. It wants to show that Shinji is alone and that they don't want to show another soul, but Fuyutsuki is absolutely necessary to leave out. The writer knows this contradiction, but did it anyway. It showed the weight and style of the scenes but it isn't necessarily consistent.

[]:             Quote Post by iihieiii » Sun Jun 23, 2013 6:39 pm ElMariachi wrote: And it's probable that Kaworu's piano mere existence follows the same rule : from where did it come? How it ended down there? It doesn't matter, what's important is that this piano, the symbol of Kaworu and Shinji's friendship and "work together"

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun Jun 23, 2013 6:51 pm 3.0 Interview are scattered left and right, there wasn't a place were most of them were gathered like the 1.0 and 2.0 CRC yet. The biggest thing we have is an interview of the Voice Actors, which casts an interesting light on how they interpreted all the changes in the characters(especially an haunting interview of how Ogata(Shinji) used the then recent earthquake and tsunami which fell on Japan to convey Shinji's feels how endless guilt and despair), which some hindsights of Anno's interpretation of Q!Asuka(the comparison with an "embittered mercenary" actually comes from him) and some interesting informations about Mari(we learn that she's a very honest girl and don't have an hidden agenda : she really works for WILLE) http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/13685/Evangelion-30-interviews-needs-translation/

[]:             Quote Post by cloudxsolder » Sun Jun 23, 2013 7:16 pm Why a piano? - It's the most fitting instrument for a duet in which the same instrument is shared, symbolising union. But I like to think past the fact that it's a piano - and think it's just music being used as a symbol of Kawuro's willingness to embrace mankind. Many would argue that music is mankind's greatest accomplishment - and he is VERY impressed with it. I would also like to think that Yamaha paid a pretty penny for it's part as well....

[]:             Quote Post by Rena » Sun Jun 23, 2013 7:40 pm Episode 24's second draft (sorry for using non-canonical information to back my point) has Shinji mention there is a piano at Nerv. Maybe there was one onsite to begin with, and Kaworu just moved it? Which poses the question as to why there is a piano in Nerv in the first place.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Mon Jun 24, 2013 3:03 am Maybe the piano was in the NERV recreation room(didn't they mention something like that in "Magma Diver" in NGE?), or in the restaurant where Gendo was eating with Rei(we see other tables, so it's not for Gendo's exclusive use), or maybe there is a reception hall and a piano is put there. For a base as big as NERV HQ, which double as the Main Branch and "heart" of the whole organization, the existence of such facilities wouldn't be that far fetched.

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Mon Jun 24, 2013 3:20 am I'm betting on the restaurant it's the only possibility that makes sense to me in Rebuild.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Mon Jun 24, 2013 3:22 am If that even is a restaurant... as I question here .

He illustrated that with the example of neo-NERV HQ and Fuyutsuki in 3.0 : Toshio Okada wrote: To me, Evangelion is a scene oriented work. It isn't a work which focuses on the scenario as its top priority. What I mean by that is that it's not a work that is meant to "show this certain story" but instead it's a work with a stockpile of "scenes to be shown" is pieced and ordered into a work. It's a dance song. You don't only have to listen to the song but see the dance moves. But the moves don't necessarily have to match with the lyrics. I believe Evangelion isn't a story where the concern went into making sure that what is shown matches the story itself, but more importantly glued well together with style and entertaining elements. In Q, Nerv, being the evil side and Wille of the justice is facing one another. But there are only two people at Nerv, Gendo and Fuyutsuki. So people wonder 'does Fuyutsuki do all the work?' But that doesn't really matter because of course it's scene oriented. It wants to show that Shinji is alone and that they don't want to show another soul, but Fuyutsuki is absolutely necessary to leave out. The writer knows this contradiction, but did it anyway. It showed the weight and style of the scenes but it isn't necessarily consistent.
